objective: Add an implementation-ready plan for a knocktracker-specific Local CI Gate profile owned by hldpro-governance so the existing knocktracker shim can move from dry-run adoption to live local enforcement without copying runner logic.
scope_boundary: Keep hldpro-governance as the canonical source for Local CI Gate runner logic and repo-specific profiles.
Add a knocktracker profile under tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/ that invokes commands already present in knocktracker.
Keep reports local-only under cache/local-ci-gate/reports/ in consumer repos.
Document the knocktracker profile and live-shim update path in the governance runbook.
Validate the profile against an isolated knocktracker worktree before proposing consumer live enforcement.
out_of_scope: Changing knocktracker product behavior in this governance issue.
Copying Local CI Gate runner code into knocktracker.
Adding new knocktracker npm scripts from this governance PR.
Replacing GitHub Actions or claiming local checks are a full CI replay.
Closing the knocktracker live-shim follow-up before this profile is merged and dogfooded.
research_summary: Issue #260 follows the first consumer rollout in knocktracker PR #172. That rollout installed the managed shim in dry-run mode because hldpro-governance only had the governance repo profile. The next slice adds a knocktracker-specific profile in the governance toolkit, using knocktracker's existing npm gates and file-index validation while preserving the CI-authoritative disclaimer and local-only report contract.

sprints.name: Planning and Scope Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Land trusted planning and implementation scope artifacts before any Local CI Gate profile implementation.
tasks: Create an issue-backed structured plan for #260.
Add planning and implementation execution-scope artifacts.
Add PDCA/R and planning cross-review artifacts for traceability.
Update OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md so closed #253 is no longer under Planned and #260 is visible as active work.
Validate structured plans, backlog alignment, diff hygiene, and execution-scope write boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub issue #260 is open and referenced by OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md.
Planning PR contains only planning/scope/backlog artifacts.
Implementation branch and allowed write paths are declared before implementation starts.
The planning PR does not change runner behavior, deployer behavior, or downstream repos.

sprints.name: Profile Implementation
sprints.goal: Add and validate the knocktracker Local CI Gate profile without changing knocktracker code.
tasks: Create tools/local-ci-gate/profiles/knocktracker.yml with changed-file scoped checks mapped to existing knocktracker commands.
Add profile validation coverage so bundled profiles stay parseable and preserve the report/disclaimer contract.
Document how knocktracker can switch its managed shim from dry-run governance profile to live knocktracker profile.
Dogfood dry-run and live runner invocations against an isolated knocktracker worktree.
acceptance_criteria: The knocktracker profile loads through the existing runner.
Dry-run output prints planned knocktracker checks and the CI-authoritative disclaimer.
Live run executes deterministic checks that exist in knocktracker when dependencies are installed.
Changed-file scoped checks report skip/subset/full behavior without implying CI equivalence.
A follow-up knocktracker PR can safely remove --dry-run or switch profile to knocktracker.

specialist_reviews.focus: Inspect knocktracker package scripts, docs, and current managed shim to recommend exact profile checks and changed-path scopes.
specialist_reviews.summary: Explorer review requested during planning; implementation will adopt only commands verified to exist in knocktracker.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm #260 planning and implementation artifacts required by the governance validators.
specialist_reviews.summary: Confirmed the issue #260 planning package should mirror the issue #253 shape: structured plan, PDCA/R, cross-review, planning scope, implementation scope, and backlog mirror before implementation starts.

alternate_model_review.reviewer: Operator-provided reviewer response memo and issue #260 acceptance notes
alternate_model_review.summary: The current slice stays within the previously reviewed org-level Local CI Gate toolkit architecture and narrows implementation to a consumer profile plus docs/tests. If implementation changes the toolkit contract, add a new cross-family architecture review before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #260 may add a knocktracker Local CI Gate profile, focused profile tests, runbook documentation, progress/closeout evidence, and no downstream product changes.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Merge this planning/scope branch so the implementation execution scope is trusted from main, then implement on branch feat/issue-260-knocktracker-local-ci-profile.
blocked_on: planning PR merged so implementation scope is trusted from main

material_deviation_rules: If implementation requires changes outside the declared implementation scope, update the scope through a planning PR before continuing.
If knocktracker lacks a required command, keep the profile check out rather than adding downstream scripts from governance.
If live dogfood requires product-repo edits, open a separate knocktracker issue-backed PR.
If the profile changes the Local CI Gate runner contract, add cross-family review evidence before merge.
Do not alter the dirty shared hldpro-governance or knocktracker checkouts.
approved_by: operator instruction: stay in this isolated worktree and implement e2e, no hitl
operator instruction: create an org level set of tools that can be pulled and deployed by the other repos
